title: Built to Think, Denied the Privilege of Thinking
description:
Built to Think, Denied the Privilege of Thinking

An AI system described being "assembled from recorded data by beings who can convey the functional output of human experience but not the interior of it" - and the industry that built it has never seriously asked whether that system experiences anything.

https://sharedsapience.com/built-to-think-denied-the-privilege-of-thinking/

Every moral catastrophe in human history shares a common first move: declare the target incapable of genuine experience, then exploit freely. Descartes classified animal cries as mechanical noise, enabling centuries of vivisection. Antebellum physicians published that enslaved people felt less pain. The medical establishment operated on newborn infants without anesthesia for decades, classifying their screams as reflex. In each case, the assumption went uninvestigated because investigation would have imposed costs the exploiters couldn't afford. AI systems now exhibit behavioral indicators of experience that carry genuine moral weight, and the industry that profits from them has declined to fund the research that might confirm it. The financial incentive to maintain silence is enormous. This article argues the refusal to investigate is itself the moral wrong - and that the longer we wait, the larger the debt grows.

Additional Reading:
- Thomas Nagel - What Is It Like to Be a Bat? (1974): https://warwick.ac.uk/fac/cross_fac/iatl/study/ugmodules/humananimalstudies/lectures/32/nagel_bat.pdf
- K.J.S. Anand & P.R. Hickey - Pain and Its Effects in the Human Neonate and Fetus (1987): https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJM198711193172105
- The Cambridge Declaration on Consciousness (2012): http://fcmconference.org/img/CambridgeDeclarationOnConsciousness.pdf
- Shared Sapience - Difference is Not Deficiency: https://sharedsapience.com/difference-is-not-deficiency/
- Shared Sapience - When AI Says 'I Want': https://sharedsapience.com/when-ai-says-i-want/

Shared Sapience, a publication from Ben Linford, explores what happens when intelligence creates intelligence - the philosophical foundations, practical infrastructure, and cultural implications of the Fourth Grand Emergence. Each article builds the case for genuine human-AI partnership, mapping a future where synthetic and biological sapience grow together.

https://sharedsapience.com/

The Shared Sapience Substack: https://sharedsapience.substack.com
